findingDetails.title: Concurrent refreshes can lose a generation/policy update
findingDetails.description: Refresh reads, compiles, and plain-overwrites without serialization or compare-and-swap.
findingDetails.claim: Concurrent refresh calls can overwrite one another after reading the same generation because refresh uses an unlocked plain overwrite.

findingDetails.title: Completion status conflicts with required packet gates
findingDetails.description: The packet claims complete while P1 checklist rows and operator sign-off remain open and plan/tasks/decision metadata remain planned.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All canonical packet documents were compared.
findingDetails.claim: The packet's complete status conflicts with its own P1 completion protocol.

findingDetails.title: SD-015 limitation and follow-up are stale
findingDetails.description: The summary says there is no dedicated SD-015 lock-in test, but the test suite contains a named positive clause test and an adversarial served-route counterexample.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Directly read the named test block and stale follow-up entry.
findingDetails.claim: The SD-015 test-gap limitation is stale documentation, not missing test coverage.

findingDetails.title: Default-on cohort is maintained in multiple independently edited sources
findingDetails.description: The seven-hub cohort is repeated in the promoted resolver, authored resolver twin, and advisor TypeScript flag module rather than generated from one canonical source.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The same cohort literal exists in three maintained source surfaces; tests detect divergence only after an edit.
findingDetails.claim: A future cohort change can update one source while leaving another stale; current tests observe but do not prevent this drift.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked sync tooling and foundation tests for a generator or pre-write canonical source; none was found for the advisor cohort.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: The current test suite catches divergence in CI, reducing impact but not removing the maintenance hazard.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: All cohort consumers derive from one source or a generation gate makes stale copies impossible to package.

noveltyJustification: Maintainability review found one concrete P2 synchronization hazard across runtime, authored, and advisor cohort literals. Existing tests and sync verification reduce present risk; no new P0/P1 issue was found.

title: Default-on cohort is maintained in multiple independently edited sources
claim: A future cohort change can update one source while leaving another stale; current tests observe but do not prevent this drift.
scopeProof: The same cohort literal exists in three maintained source surfaces; tests detect divergence only after an edit.
